lobe.name: Svrn7.Calendar
lobe.title: SVRN7 Calendar LOBE
lobe.description: DIDComm-native calendar using iCalendar (RFC 5545) tunneling. Receives event publications and meeting invites; sends RSVP replies. ATTENDEE properties use did:drn URIs. No CalDAV server.
lobe.author: Michael Herman

protocols.title: Calendar Event Publication
protocols.description: Publishes a VCALENDAR event to a peer TDA. Body: { from: did, icalendarBody: string }

protocols.title: Meeting Invite (METHOD:REQUEST)
protocols.description: iCalendar METHOD:REQUEST meeting invite sent to attendees.

protocols.title: Meeting RSVP (METHOD:REPLY)
protocols.description: iCalendar METHOD:REPLY accept or decline from an attendee.

cmdlets.name: Import-TdaCalendarEvent
cmdlets.title: Import Calendar Event
cmdlets.description: Processes an inbound calendar/1.0/event. Extracts iCalendar fields, maps ATTENDEE did:drn URIs, returns a calendar event record.

MessageDid.description: TDA resource DID URL of the inbox message. Form: did:drn:{networkId}/inbox/msg/{objectId}

OrganizerDid.description: DID of the event organiser (from DIDComm envelope).

cmdlets.name: Receive-TdaMeetingRequest
cmdlets.title: Receive Meeting Invite
cmdlets.description: Processes an inbound calendar/1.0/invite (METHOD:REQUEST). Returns a pending invite record. Pipe to New-TdaCalendarResponse to accept or decline.

MessageDid.description: TDA resource DID URL of the inbox message. Form: did:drn:{networkId}/inbox/msg/{objectId}

OrganizerDid.description: DID of the event organiser (from DIDComm envelope).

cmdlets.name: New-TdaCalendarResponse
cmdlets.title: Send Meeting RSVP
cmdlets.description: Generates a calendar/1.0/response (METHOD:REPLY) for a meeting invite. Switch -Accept or -Decline. Returns OutboundMessage.

PeerEndpoint.description: HTTP/2 endpoint URL of the target TDA.

PackedMessage.description: JSON-serialised DIDComm message body (not yet packed — Switchboard packs before delivery).

MessageType.description: DIDComm @type URI of the outbound message.

ai._note: Epoch 0: these fields are informational for human and AI developers. inputSchema/outputSchema on each cmdlet are optional in Epoch 0. In a future epoch, an AI pipeline composer will consume this descriptor directly — possibly via an MCP-compatible tools/list interface — to discover, understand, and dynamically compose LOBE pipelines. The LOBE descriptor will become the MCP tool definition with no translation needed.
ai.summary: Handles iCalendar event exchange and meeting scheduling between TDA citizens using DIDComm. No CalDAV. Attendees are identified by did:drn DIDs.
useCases: Sending meeting invites to citizens in the same or different Society
Publishing calendar events to peer TDAs
Processing RSVP replies and updating local event records
compositionHints: For invite handling, chain: Get-TdaMessage | Receive-TdaMeetingRequest | New-TdaCalendarResponse | Send-TdaMessage
For event publication, use Import-TdaCalendarEvent which handles both events and RSVP replies
Check MessageType before routing: calendar/1.0/invite → Receive-TdaMeetingRequest; all others → Import-TdaCalendarEvent
limitations: Epoch 0: no recurring event support. Single VEVENT per VCALENDAR.
No free/busy query protocol in Epoch 0.
